race_desc: Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election in Alabama since 1998.
Alaska has a very interesting race with Democrat Tom Begich, part of a well known family in Alaska, running for governor in this open seat.
Incumbent Katie Hubbs is running for a second term in a state that Trump won back in 2024.
Incumbent Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders is seeking re-election to a second term.
California hasn't elected a Republican for Governor since Arnold Schwarzenegger in 2006.
Colorado is a reliable Democrat state and Republicans have not won a gubernatorial election in Colorado since Bill Owens was re-elected in 2002.
Incumbent Governor Ned Lamont is running for a third term.
The race to succeed Incumbent Governor Ron DeSantis will be an interesting one in this once swing state now trending strongly towards Republicans.
Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election in Georgia since 1998. This year might be their best shot yet to break that streak.
Republicans have not won a gubernatorial eelction in Hawaii since Linda Lingle was re-elected in 2006.
Incumbent Governor Brad Little is running for a third term. Democrats have not won the governership here since 1990.
Incumbent Governor JB Pritzker is running for a third term.
This is surprisingly the first open seat gubernatorial election in the state since 2006, which as also the last gubernatorial election in Iowa won by a Democrat.
Democrat Governor Laura Kelly is term limited in this typically Republican state.
Maine is a reliably Democrat state although it currenlty has one Republican senator.
Incumbent Governor Wes Moore is running for a second term.
Incumbent Governor Maura Healey is running for a second term.
This race is unique as it features a decently strong Independent Candidate in former Detroit Mayor Mike Duggan.
Incumbent Governor Tim Walz is not running for re-election, leaving the seat an open race in this reliably Democrat state.
Incumbent Governor Jim Pillen is seeking re-eleciton to a second term. Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election in Nebraska since 1994.
Incumbent Governor Joe Lombardo is running for re-election to a second term in office in this swing state.
This is one of two Republican-held governorships up for election in a state that Kamala Harris won in the 2024 Presidential Election.
New Mexico hasn't elected a Republican governor since 2014.
Incumbent Governor Kathy Hochul is running for her second full term. Hochul took office after her predecessor Andrew Cuomo resigned in 2021.
Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election in Ohio since 2006 and have not won statewide since 2020. This race may be the Democrats best chance to break that streak this year.
Oklahoma is a deeply red state where Democrats have not won statewide since 2006.
Incumbent Governor Tina Kotek is running for a second full term.
Incumbent Governor Josh Shapiro is running for a second term.
Incumbent Governor Dan McKee is running for a second full term in office. McKee became governor in 2021 upon the resignation of Gina Raimondo.
South Carolina has an open seat. Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election here since 1998.

Incumbent Governor Larry Rhoden, who ascended to the office in 2025 after Kristi Noem resigned to become secretary of homeland security, is running for his first full term in office. Rhoden had previously been serving as lieutenant governor under Noem since the two took office in 2019.[2] Democrats have not won a gubernatorial election in South Dakota since 1974.
This deep red state has an open governor seat. Democrats have not won a statewide election in Tennessee since 2006.
Incumbent Governor Greg Abbott is running for a fourth term.
Phil Scott has been governor since 2016 and is currently the most popular state governor in the nation according to a Morning Consult poll.
Wisconsin has an open race which will be closely watched after incumbent Governor Tony Evers decided not to run for re-election.
Democrats have not won statewide in Wyoming since 2006.
